page.title: Integrations
page.subtitle: Manage API keys for CI/CD scanning and configure repository webhooks.

api_keys.section_description: Use these tokens with the GitHub Action / GitLab template / Jenkins pipeline. Revoke immediately if a key is exposed.
api_keys.empty: No API keys yet. Create one to get started.

revoke_confirm.title: Revoke this API key?
revoke_confirm.description: This is immediate and cannot be undone. CI runners using this key will start failing.

create_dialog.title: Create a new API key
create_dialog.description: The plaintext key is shown only once after creation — capture it then.
create_dialog.scope_help_org: Visible to the whole organization. Super admin only.

create_result.title: API key created
create_result.warning: This is the only time the key is shown. Store it securely — losing it means you must rotate the key.

errors.team_id_required: Team ID is required for team-scoped keys.
errors.project_id_required: Project ID is required for project-scoped keys.
errors.create_failed: Failed to create the API key.
errors.revoke_failed: Failed to revoke the API key.
errors.copy_failed: Could not copy to clipboard. Select the text and copy manually.

webhooks.section_description: Point your repository's webhook configuration to one of these URLs. The shared secret is the project's webhook_secret (set it in the project's Settings tab).

gitlab.label: GitLab
gitlab.header: Header: X-Gitlab-Token (bearer secret, sent as plaintext)
